data.acceptance:
1. On /beats?beatsView=queues in multi-select mode, a bulk tag/label control is available that calls onBulkUpdate with { labels: string[] } (and optionally { removeLabels: string[] }) applied to every id in selectedIds.
2. Applying labels to N selected beats persists to all N beats, verified both in the beat-detail UI after reload and via kno ls --json on the underlying knots.
3. The per-field bulk editors (Type, Priority, Profile, State, Tags) are consolidated into a single grouped surface (e.g. an "Edit fields" popover/menu) instead of separate always-visible inline dropdowns; the primary actions (Ship, Scene!, Merge, Refine Scope, Clear, and the "N selected" count) remain directly visible.
4. With all controls present, the multi-select bar fits without horizontal-scroll at >= 768px viewport width (no overflow-x scrolling required to reach Apply or Clear).
5. The tag control accepts multiple labels, displays the pending labels before Apply, and rejects empty/whitespace-only labels.
6. No new hardcoded workflow-state strings are introduced, and bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test, and bun run build all pass.
